description_long: This object is a small, rustic graveyard structure with a steeply pitched, thatched roof and a dark, weathered wooden exterior. The main building appears to be a chapel or mausoleum, with a smaller annex attached to one side, both featuring simple, rectangular forms. The roof is covered in brownish, straw-like material, giving it an aged, rural appearance, while the walls are constructed from dark timber planks. Sparse vegetation and overgrown grass are visible around the base, suggesting a neglected or historic site. No visible inscriptions, crosses, or ornate grave markers are present, and the structure is compact, likely intended for a single family or small group. The overall impression is of a modest, old-world graveyard building, blending into a natural, possibly forested environment.
description: A small, rustic graveyard building with a thatched roof and dark wooden walls, surrounded by sparse vegetation. The structure appears aged and simple, with a main section and a smaller annex.
description_view_0: The main building and annex are visible, with the thatched roof and dark wooden walls prominent; the annex is more clearly seen from this angle.
description_view_1: A side view showing the narrow profile of the structure, emphasizing the height and the overhanging thatched roof.
description_view_2: The opposite side of the main building is visible, with the annex less prominent and the roof's texture more apparent.
description_view_3: A rear or frontal view of the structure, highlighting the vertical lines of the wooden planks and the steep pitch of the roof.
